is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum malaria: 'Among 903 people with P. falciparum malaria' and keywords include Plasmodium falciparum.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfk13 (R622I, C580Y), Pfcrt (K76T, N75E, A220S, N326S, R371I), Pfmdr1 86 (N86), Pfdhfr (N51I, C59R, S108N), Pfdhps (A437G, K540E, A581G).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Explicit frequencies/counts: 'R622I mutation was found in 369/833 samples (44.3%)'; dhfr N51I 99.4%, S108N 99.2%, C59R 31.1%; dhps A437G 88.8%, K540E 89.1%, A581G 5.0%; crt CVIET 98.1%, A220S 84.9%, N326S 76.4%, R371I 92.5%; mdr1 N86 99.8%.

sub_country_location.evidence: Two districts Gondar Zuria and Tach Armachiho, Amhara region, with 26 named Kebeles (villages) and coordinates used for rainfall; Figure S3 gives per-Kebele prevalence.

collection_window_within_3y.evidence: 'The study was conducted from November 01, 2022 to October 31, 2023' - a single year.

african_field_samples.evidence: Human field/clinical isolates collected in northwest Ethiopia from microscopically confirmed P. falciparum patients at health centers.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary genomic surveillance study with MIP and WGS sequencing of collected samples.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Same research group (Bailey/Fola/Brhane) has multiple Ethiopia Amhara studies (Fola et al. 2023; Brhane et al. 2024). This is described as new in-depth sampling for 2022-2023, distinct window, but part of ongoing surveillance in overlapping region; funded via EACCR consortium and NIH R01AI177791.

supplement.evidence: Per-locus and per-Kebele frequency counts are in supplementary tables not provided: 'Table S1' (overall), 'Table S2' (per district), 'Table S3' (per Kebele), and site-level finer detail; 'available in the supplementary tables.'

assessment.summary: Primary genomic surveillance study of 903 P. falciparum patients collected over one year (Nov 2022-Oct 2023) in two districts (Gondar Zuria, Tach Armachiho), Amhara region, Ethiopia, with sub-country resolution down to 26 Kebeles. Reports extractable frequencies for numerous curated markers in Pfk13, Pfcrt, Pfmdr1, Pfdhfr, and Pfdhps. Eligible on all criteria. Detailed per-locus and per-site tables are in supplementary materials not provided.
